Kikuyu: Nĩ niĩ Paũlũ, o niĩ ndeetirwo nduĩke mũtũmwo wa Kristũ Jesũ nĩ ũndũ wa kwenda kwa Ngai, ndĩratũma ngeithi ici tũrĩ hamwe na mũrũ wa Ithe witũ Sosithene,*
English: Paul, called to be an apostle of Jesus Christ† through the will of God, and our brother Sosthenes,

Kikuyu: Twandĩkĩra kanitha wa Ngai ũrĩa ũrĩ Korinitho, kũrĩ arĩa othe maamũrĩtwo thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ na magetwo matuĩke andũ atheru, o hamwe na arĩa othe marĩ kũndũ guothe makayagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ, o we Mwathani wao na witũ:
English: to the assembly of God which is at Corinth—those who are sanctified in Christ Jesus, called saints, with all who call on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place, both theirs and ours:

Kikuyu: Wega na thayũ irogĩa kũrĩ inyuĩ ciumĩte kũrĩ Ngai Ithe witũ na Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ.
English: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

Kikuyu: Nĩnjookagĩria Ngai ngaatho hĩndĩ ciothe nĩ ũndũ wanyu, nĩ ũndũ wa wega wake ũrĩa mũheetwo thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ.
English: I always thank my God concerning you for the grace of God which was given you in Christ Jesus,

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo thĩinĩ wake nĩmũtongetio maũndũ-inĩ mothe, mũgatongio ũhoro- inĩ wa mĩario yanyu yothe o na ũhoro-inĩ wothe wa kũmenya maũndũ,
English: that in everything you were enriched in him, in all speech and all knowledge—

Kikuyu: tondũ-rĩ, ũira witũ ũkoniĩ Kristũ nĩwekĩrirwo hinya thĩinĩ wanyu.
English: even as the testimony of Christ was confirmed in you—

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio gũtirĩ kĩheo gĩa kĩĩroho o na kĩmwe mũtarĩ nakĩo, o mũgĩetagĩrĩra na kĩyo kũguũranĩrio kwa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ.
English: so that you come behind in no gift, waiting for the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ,

Kikuyu: Nake nĩarĩmũikaragia mũrĩ na hinya nginya mũthia, nĩguo mũtikanakorwo mũrĩ na ũcuuke mũthenya ũrĩa wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ.
English: who will also confirm you until the end, blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩ mwĩhokeku, o we ũrĩa wamwĩtire nĩguo mũkorwo na ngwatanĩro na Mũriũ Jesũ Kristũ, Mwathani witũ.
English: God is faithful, through whom you were called into the fellowship of his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, inyuĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, ndamũthaitha thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ, atĩ inyuothe mũiguane o mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ, nĩguo mũtikagĩe na nyamũkano gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu, no mũikare mũiguanĩte, na mũrĩ na meciiria o mamwe, na ngoro o ĩmwe.
English: Now I beg you, brothers,‡ through the name of our Lord, Jesus Christ, that you all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be perfected together in the same mind and in the same judgment.

Kikuyu: Tondũ rĩĩrĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, nĩmenyithĩtio nĩ andũ amwe a kuuma nyũmba ya Kiloe atĩ nĩ kũrĩ na ngũĩ gatagatĩ- inĩ kanyu.
English: For it has been reported to me concerning you, my brothers, by those who are from Chloe’s household, that there are contentions among you.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa ndĩroiga nĩ atĩrĩ: Ũmwe wanyu oigaga atĩ, “Niĩ nũmagĩrĩra Paũlũ”; ũngĩ akoiga atĩ, “Niĩ nũmagĩrĩra Apolo”; nake ũngĩ akoiga atĩ, “Niĩ nũmagĩrĩra Kefa”; o na ningĩ ũngĩ akoiga atĩ, “Niĩ nũmagĩrĩra Kristũ.”
English: Now I mean this, that each one of you says, “I follow Paul,” “I follow Apollos,” “I follow Cephas,” and, “I follow Christ.”

Kikuyu: Kaĩ Kristũ agayanĩtio? Paũlũ-rĩ, nĩwe waambirwo mũtĩ igũrũ nĩ ũndũ wanyu? Mwabatithirio thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Paũlũ?
English: Is Christ divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized into the name of Paul?

Kikuyu: Nĩngũcookia ngaatho nĩ ũndũ gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wanyu niĩ ndaabatithirie, o tiga Kirisipo na Gayo.
English: I thank God that I baptized none of you except Crispus and Gaius,

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe ũngiuga atĩ mwabatithirio thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa.
English: so that no one should say that I had baptized you into my own name.

Kikuyu: Ĩĩ, o na ningĩ nĩndabatithirie andũ a nyũmba ya Stefana; no tiga acio, ndingĩririkana kana nĩ kũrĩ mũndũ ũngĩ ndaabatithirie.
English: (I also baptized the household of Stephanas; besides them, I don’t know whether I baptized any other.)

Kikuyu: Nĩ gũkorwo Kristũ ndaandũmire ngabatithanie, no aandũmire ngahunjie Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega, no ti ndĩũheane na ũũgĩ wa mũndũ, nĩgeetha mũtharaba wa Kristũ ndũkae kwagithio kĩene.
English: For Christ sent me not to baptize, but to preach the Good News—not in wisdom of words, so that the cross of Christ wouldn’t be made void.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ndũmĩrĩri ya mũtharaba nĩ ũrimũ kũrĩ arĩa maroora, no kũrĩ ithuĩ arĩa tũrahonokio- rĩ, ũndũ ũcio nĩ hinya wa Ngai.
English: For the word of the cross is foolishness to those who are dying, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Ũũgĩ wa andũ arĩa oogĩ nĩkũniina ngaaũniina; naguo ũmenyo wa andũ arĩa imenyi ndĩkĩũtue wa tũhũ.”
English: For it is written, “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise. I will bring the discernment of the discerning to nothing.”*

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mũũgĩ-rĩ, akĩrĩ kũ? Mũndũ ũrĩa mũthomu-rĩ, akĩrĩ kũ? Arĩ ha mũciiri ũrĩa ũũĩ gũciira maũndũ ma mahinda maya tũrĩ? Githĩ Ngai ndatuĩte ũũgĩ wa thĩ ĩno ũrimũ?
English: Where is the wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the debater of this age? Hasn’t God made foolish the wisdom of this world?

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Ngai na ũndũ wa ũũgĩ wake nĩonire atĩ andũ a thĩ ĩno na ũũgĩ wao matikaamũmenya, akĩona kwagĩrĩire ahũthĩre maũndũ ma ũrimũ marĩa tũhunjagia nĩgeetha ahonokie arĩa mamwĩtĩkagia.
English: For seeing that in the wisdom of God, the world through its wisdom didn’t know God, it was God’s good pleasure through the foolishness of the preaching to save those who believe.

Kikuyu: Ayahudi metagia ciama, nao Ayunani macaragia ũũgĩ,
English: For Jews ask for signs, Greeks seek after wisdom,

Kikuyu: no ithuĩ tũhunjagia ũhoro wa Kristũ arĩ mwambe mũtĩ igũrũ: maũndũ macio nĩ mũhĩnga kũrĩ Ayahudi na ũrimũ kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ,
English: but we preach Christ crucified, a stumbling block to Jews and foolishness to Greeks,

Kikuyu: no kũrĩ arĩa Ngai etĩte, Ayahudi o na Ayunani, Kristũ nĩwe hinya wa Ngai na ũũgĩ wa Ngai.
English: but to those who are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God;

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũrimũ wa Ngai nĩ mũũgĩ gũkĩra ũũgĩ wa mũndũ, naguo ũhũthũ wa Ngai nĩ mũritũ gũkĩra hinya wa mũndũ.
English: because the foolishness of God is wiser than men, and the weakness of God is stronger than men.

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, ta gĩciiriei ũrĩa mwatariĩ rĩrĩa mwetirwo. Ti aingĩ anyu oogĩ na ũũgĩ wa andũ a gũkũ thĩ; na ti aingĩ mangĩaguucĩrĩirie andũ; o na ti aingĩ maciarĩirwo mĩciĩ ĩrĩa ĩrĩ igweta.
English: For you see your calling, brothers, that not many are wise according to the flesh, not many mighty, and not many noble;

Kikuyu: NoNgaiaathuuriremaũndũmarĩamaũrimũmathĩĩnonĩguoaconorithieandũarĩaoogĩ; ningĩNgai aathuurire maũndũ marĩa matarĩ hinya ma thĩ ĩno nĩguo aconorithie arĩa marĩ hinya.
English: but God chose the foolish things of the world that he might put to shame those who are wise. God chose the weak things of the world that he might put to shame the things that are strong.

Kikuyu: Agĩthuura maũndũ marĩa mahũthũ ma thĩ ĩno, na marĩa matarĩ ma gũtĩĩka, o gwata marĩa matarĩ maũndũ kũrĩ, nĩgeetha amaagithie bata,
English: God chose the lowly things of the world, and the things that are despised, and the things that don’t exist, that he might bring to nothing the things that exist,

Kikuyu: nĩgeetha mũndũ o na ũ ndakanetĩĩe mbere ya Ngai.
English: that no flesh should boast before God.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, nĩwe ũtũmĩte mũkorwo mũrĩ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ, ũrĩa ũtuĩkĩte ũũgĩ uumĩte kũrĩ Ngai nĩ ũndũ witũ, ũguo nĩ kuuga, ũthingu witũ, na ũtheru, na gũkũũrwo.
English: Because of him, you are in Christ Jesus, who was made to us wisdom from God, and righteousness and sanctification, and redemption,

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Mũndũ ũrĩa ũkwĩraha, nĩerahage nĩ ũndũ wa Mwathani.”
English: that, as it is written, “He who boasts, let him boast in the Lord.”*

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndookire kũrĩ inyuĩ, ngĩmũhunjĩria ũira wa hitho ya ũhoro wa Ngai, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, ndiamwarĩirie na mĩario ya kanua kega kana ũũgĩ mũnene.
English: When I came to you, brothers, I didn’t come with excellence of speech or of wisdom, proclaiming to you the testimony of God.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩndatuĩte itua ndirĩ ũndũ ũngĩ ngwenda kũmenya, rĩrĩa rĩothe ndaarĩ na inyuĩ, o tiga ũhoro wa Jesũ Kristũ na kwambwo gwake mũtĩ-igũrũ.
English: For I determined not to know anything among you except Jesus Christ and him crucified.

Kikuyu: Ndookire kũrĩ inyuĩ itarĩ na hinya, na ndĩ na guoya, o na ngĩinainaga mũno.
English: I was with you in weakness, in fear, and in much trembling.

Kikuyu: Ndũmĩrĩri yakwa na kũhunjia gwakwa itiarĩ na ciugo cia kũguucanĩrĩria na ũũgĩ, no nĩcionanagia hinya wa Roho,
English: My speech and my preaching were not in persuasive words of human wisdom, but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power,

Kikuyu: nĩguo wĩtĩkio wanyu ndũgakorwo ũtiirĩrĩirwo nĩ ũũgĩ wa andũ, o tiga hinya wa Ngai.
English: that your faith wouldn’t stand in the wisdom of men, but in the power of God.

Kikuyu: No ningĩ ithuĩ-rĩ, tũrĩ gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ arĩa agima twaragia ũhoro wa ndũmĩrĩri ya ũũgĩ, no tũtiaragia ũhoro wa ũũgĩ wa mahinda maya, kana wa aathani a mahinda maya arĩa matarĩ kũrĩa merekeire.
English: We speak wisdom, however, among those who are full grown, yet a wisdom not of this world nor of the rulers of this world who are coming to nothing.

Kikuyu: No ithuĩ-rĩ, twaragia ũhoro wa ũũgĩ wa Ngai ũrĩa wa hitho, ũũgĩ ũrĩa watũire ũrĩ mũhithe, o ũrĩa Ngai aathĩrĩire o mbere ya kũũmba thĩ nĩ ũndũ wa riiri witũ.
English: But we speak God’s wisdom in a mystery, the wisdom that has been hidden, which God foreordained before the worlds for our glory,

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wa anene a mahinda maya wamenyire ũhoro ũcio, nĩgũkorwo mangĩaũmenyete-rĩ, matingĩaambire Mwathani wa riiri.
English: which none of the rulers of this world has known. For had they known it, they wouldn’t have crucified the Lord of glory.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo no ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Maũndũ marĩa matarĩ monwo na maitho, na matarĩ maiguuo na matũ, o na matarĩ maatoonya ngoro ya mũndũ, nĩmo Ngai athondekeire andũ arĩa mamwendete”:
English: But as it is written, “Things which an eye didn’t see, and an ear didn’t hear, which didn’t enter into the heart of man, these God has prepared for those who love him.”*

Kikuyu: no Ngai nĩatũguũrĩirie maũndũ macio na ũndũ wa Roho wake. Roho nĩathuthuuragia maũndũ mothe, o na maũndũ ma Ngai marĩa marikĩru mũno.
English: But to us, God revealed them through the Spirit. For the Spirit searches all things, yes, the deep things of God.

Kikuyu: Tondũ- rĩ, nũũ ũũĩ meciiria ma mũndũ, tiga o roho wa mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ thĩinĩ wake? O ta ũguo-rĩ, gũtirĩ mũndũ ũũĩ meciiria ma Ngai, o tiga Roho wa Ngai.
English: For who among men knows the things of a man except the spirit of the man which is in him? Even so, no one knows the things of God except God’s Spirit.

Kikuyu: Tũtiamũkĩrĩte roho wa thĩ ĩno, no tũheetwo Roho ũrĩa uumĩte kũrĩ Ngai, nĩguo tũmenyage iheo iria Ngai atũheete o ũguo tũhũ.
English: But we received not the spirit of the world, but the Spirit which is from God, that we might know the things that were freely given to us by God.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ macio nĩmo twaragia, no tũtimaaragia na ciugo iria tũrutĩtwo na ũũgĩ wa mũndũ, no nĩ na ciugo iria tũrutagwo nĩ Roho, tũgataarĩria ũhoro wa ma wa kĩĩroho na ciugo cia kĩĩroho.
English: We also speak these things, not in words which man’s wisdom teaches but which the Holy Spirit teaches, comparing spiritual things with spiritual things.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũtarĩ na Roho ndetĩkagĩra maũndũ marĩa moimaga kũrĩ Roho wa Ngai, tondũ maũndũ macio nĩ ũrimũ kũrĩ we, na ndangĩmamenya, tondũ makũũrĩkanaga na njĩra ya kĩĩroho.
English: Now the natural man doesn’t receive the things of God’s Spirit, for they are foolishness to him; and he can’t know them, because they are spiritually discerned.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ wa kĩĩroho nĩakũũranaga maũndũ mothe, nowe mwene gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩmũtuĩra:
English: But he who is spiritual discerns all things, and he himself is to be judged by no one.

Kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩmenya meciiria ma Mwathani, nĩguo akĩmũtaare?” No ithuĩ tũrĩ na meciiria ma Kristũ.
English: “For who has known the mind of the Lord that he should instruct him?”* But we have Christ’s mind.

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, ndingĩahotire kũmwarĩria ta mũrĩ andũ a kĩĩroho, no ndaamwarĩirie ta mũrĩ andũ a thĩ, o ta twana tũnini thĩinĩ wa Kristũ.
English: Brothers, I couldn’t speak to you as to spiritual, but as to fleshly, as to babies in Christ.

Kikuyu: Ndaamũheire iria, no ti irio nyũmũ ndaamũheire, nĩgũkorwo mũtiakinyĩte a gũcirĩa. Ti-itherũ o na rĩu mũtikinyĩte a gũcirĩa.
English: I fed you with milk, not with solid food, for you weren’t yet ready. Indeed, you aren’t ready even now,

Kikuyu: O na rĩu mũrĩ andũ a thĩ. Kuona atĩ kũrĩ na ũiru na ngũĩ thĩinĩ wanyu-rĩ, githĩ mũtikĩrĩ andũ a thĩ? Githĩ mũtiragĩĩka o ta ũrĩa andũ a ndũire mekaga?
English: for you are still fleshly. For insofar as there is jealousy, strife, and factions among you, aren’t you fleshly, and don’t you walk in the ways of men?

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, rĩrĩa mũndũ ũmwe ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nũmagĩrĩra Paũlũ,” nake ũrĩa ũngĩ akoiga atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nũmagĩrĩra Apolo,” githĩ mũtikĩrĩ o andũ a ndũire?
English: For when one says, “I follow Paul,” and another, “I follow Apollos,” aren’t you fleshly?

Kikuyu: Kaĩ Apolo akĩrĩ ũ? Nake Paũlũ akĩrĩ ũ? Acio maarĩ o ndungata, iria ciatũmire mwĩtĩkie, o ta ũrĩa Mwathani aagaĩire o mũndũ wĩra wake.
English: Who then is Apollos, and who is Paul, but servants through whom you believed, and each as the Lord gave to him?

Kikuyu: Niĩ nĩ mbeũ ndahaandire, nake Apolo agĩciitĩrĩria maaĩ, no nĩ Ngai watũmire ikũre.
English: I planted. Apollos watered. But God gave the increase.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ti mũndũ ũrĩa ũhaandaga kana ũrĩa ũitagĩrĩria maaĩ ũrĩ bata, no ũrĩa ũrĩ bata nĩ Ngai, ũrĩa ũtũmaga ikũre.
English: So then neither he who plants is anything, nor he who waters, but God who gives the increase.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhaandaga na mũndũ ũrĩa ũitagĩrĩria maaĩ makoragwo na muoroto ũmwe, na o mũndũ akaarĩhwo kũringana na wĩra wake we mwene.
English: Now he who plants and he who waters are the same, but each will receive his own reward according to his own labor.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo tũrutithanagia wĩra hamwe na Ngai; na inyuĩ mũrĩ mũgũnda wa Ngai, na mwako wa Ngai.
English: For we are God’s fellow workers. You are God’s farming, God’s building.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa wega ũrĩa Ngai aaheete, niĩ nĩ gwaka ndaakire mũthingi haana ta mwakithia ũrĩa mũũgĩ nake mũndũ ũngĩ nĩ gwaka araaka igũrũ rĩa mũthingi ũcio. No o mũndũ nĩamenyagĩrĩre ũrĩa egwaka igũrũ rĩaguo.
English: According to the grace of God which was given to me, as a wise master builder I laid a foundation, and another builds on it. But let each man be careful how he builds on it.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩaka mũthingi ũngĩ tiga ũrĩa ũrĩkĩtie gwakwo, na nĩguo Jesũ Kristũ.
English: For no one can lay any other foundation than that which has been laid, which is Jesus Christ.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo mũndũ o na ũ egwaka igũrũ rĩa mũthingi ũyũ akĩhũthagĩra thahabu, kana betha, kana mahiga ma goro, kana mbaũ, kana nyeki, o na kana rũũa-rĩ,
English: But if anyone builds on the foundation with gold, silver, costly stones, wood, hay, or straw,

Kikuyu: wĩra wake nĩũkoneka ũrĩa ũtariĩ, tondũ Mũthenya ũcio nĩũgaatũma woneke ũtheri-inĩ. Wĩra ũcio ũkaaguũrio na mwaki, nĩgũkorwo wĩra wa o mũndũ nĩũkagerio na mwaki ũcio.
English: each man’s work will be revealed. For the Day will declare it, because it is revealed in fire; and the fire itself will test what sort of work each man’s work is.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo kĩrĩa mũndũ aakĩte mũthingi- igũrũ nĩgĩgetiiria mwaki ũcio-rĩ, nĩakarĩhwo mũcaara wake.
English: If any man’s work remains which he built on it, he will receive a reward.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo nĩũkahĩa-rĩ, nĩakoorwo nĩguo; nowe mwene nĩakahonoka, no akaahonoka ta mũndũ ũgereire mwaki-inĩ.
English: If any man’s work is burned, he will suffer loss, but he himself will be saved, but as through fire.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ inyuĩ mũtooĩ atĩ inyuĩ ene mũrĩ hekarũ ya Ngai, na atĩ Roho wa Ngai atũũraga thĩinĩ wanyu?
English: Don’t you know that you are God’s temple and that God’s Spirit lives in you?

Kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe angĩananga hekarũ ya Ngai-rĩ, Ngai nĩakamwananga; nĩgũkorwo hekarũ ya Ngai nĩ theru, na nĩ inyuĩ hekarũ ĩyo.
English: If anyone destroys God’s temple, God will destroy him; for God’s temple is holy, which you are.

Kikuyu: Mũtikae kwĩheenia. Mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ thĩinĩ wanyu angĩkorwo nĩeciiragia nĩ mũũgĩ na ũũgĩ wa mahinda maya tũrĩ-rĩ, nĩagĩtuĩke “kĩrimũ” nĩguo atuĩke mũũgĩ.
English: Let no one deceive himself. If anyone thinks that he is wise among you in this world, let him become a fool that he may become wise.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũũgĩ wa thĩ ĩno nĩ ũrimũ maitho-inĩ ma Ngai. O ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ, “Nĩanyiitaga arĩa oogĩ waara-inĩ wao”;
English: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, “He has taken the wise in their craftiness.”*

Kikuyu: o na ningĩ nĩ kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ, “Mwathani nĩoĩ atĩ meciiria ma arĩa oogĩ nĩ ma tũhũ.”
English: And again, “The Lord knows the reasoning of the wise, that it is worthless.”*

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ndakae kwĩraha na ũhoro wa andũ! Maũndũ mothe nĩ manyu,
English: Therefore let no one boast in men. For all things are yours,

Kikuyu: kana nĩ Paũlũ, kana nĩ Apolo, kana nĩ Kefa, kana nĩ thĩ ĩno, kana nĩ muoyo, kana nĩ gĩkuũ, kana nĩ maũndũ marĩa marĩ ho rĩu, kana nĩ maũndũ marĩa magooka, mothe nĩ manyu,
English: whether Paul, or Apollos, or Cephas, or the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come. All are yours,

Kikuyu: na inyuĩ mũrĩ a Kristũ, nake Kristũ nĩ wa Ngai.
English: and you are Christ’s, and Christ is God’s.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, andũ magĩrĩirwo nĩgũtuonaga ta ndungata cia Kristũ, na ta tũrĩ andũ arĩa mehokeirwo maũndũ ma hitho ma Ngai.
English: So let a man think of us as Christ’s servants and stewards of God’s mysteries.

Kikuyu: Rĩu nĩkũbataranĩtie atĩ andũ arĩa mehokeirwo maũndũ no nginya monanie atĩ nĩ ehokeku.
English: Here, moreover, it is required of stewards that they be found faithful.

Kikuyu: Ndingĩmaka o na hanini ingĩtuĩrwo ciira nĩ inyuĩ, kana nduĩrwo nĩ igooti rĩa andũ o na rĩrĩkũ; ti-itherũ o na niĩ mwene ndingĩĩtuĩra ciira.
English: But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged by you, or by a human court. Yes, I don’t even judge my own self.

Kikuyu: Niĩ mwene ndirĩ na ũndũ ingĩĩcuukĩra, no ũndũ ũcio ndũngĩtũma nduĩke atĩ ndirĩ na mahĩtia. Mwathani nĩwe ũnduagĩra ciira.
English: For I know nothing against myself. Yet I am not justified by this, but he who judges me is the Lord.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndũkae gũtua ciira ihinda rĩrĩa rĩamũre rĩtakinyĩte; eterera nginya Mwathani ooke. Maũndũ marĩa mahithe nduma-inĩ nĩakamoimĩria ũtheri-inĩ, na nĩakaguũria meciiria ma ngoro cia andũ. Ihinda rĩu o mũndũ nĩakamũkĩra ngaatho ciake kuuma kũrĩ Ngai.
English: Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord comes, who will both bring to light the hidden things of darkness and reveal the counsels of the hearts. Then each man will get his praise from God.

Kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, maũndũ macio ndĩramaringithania na ũhoro wakwa mwene na wa Apolo nĩguo mũgunĩke, nĩguo mwĩrute na ithuĩ gĩtũmi kĩa ũrĩa kwĩragwo atĩrĩ, “Mũtikae kwagarara ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo.” Nĩgeetha mũtikae gwĩtĩĩa mũndũ ũmwe makĩria ya ũrĩa ũngĩ.
English: Now these things, brothers, I have in a figure transferred to myself and Apollos for your sakes, that in us you might learn not to think beyond the things which are written, that none of you be puffed up against one another.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, nũũ ũtũmĩte wĩone ũrĩ na ngũũrani na mũndũ ũngĩ o wothe? Nĩ kĩĩ ũrĩ nakĩo ũtaaheirwo? Na angĩkorwo nĩwaheirwo-rĩ, ũragĩĩtĩĩa nĩkĩ ta atarĩ kũheo waheirwo?
English: For who makes you different? And what do you have that you didn’t receive? But if you did receive it, why do you boast as if you had not received it?

Kikuyu: Nĩmũrĩkĩtie kũgĩa na kĩrĩa gĩothe mwendaga! Nĩmũrĩkĩtie gũtonga! Nĩmũtuĩkĩte athamaki, ithuĩ tũtarĩ ho! Naarĩ korwo ti-itherũ nĩmwatuĩkire athamaki kũna nĩguo tũtuĩke a gũthamaka hamwe na inyuĩ!
English: You are already filled. You have already become rich. You have come to reign without us. Yes, and I wish that you did reign, that we also might reign with you!

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ndĩrona atĩ ithuĩ atũmwo Ngai atũigĩte handũ tũrĩonekaga tũrĩ thuutha wa andũ arĩa angĩ othe, na ta andũ matuĩrĩirwo kũũragĩrwo thĩinĩ wa kĩhaaro kĩa mathako. Nĩtũtuĩtwo kĩndũ gĩa kwĩrorerwo nĩ thĩ yothe, kũrĩ araika o na kũrĩ andũ.
English: For I think that God has displayed us, the apostles, last of all, like men sentenced to death. For we are made a spectacle to the world, both to angels and men.

Kikuyu: Ithuĩ tũrĩ irimũ nĩ ũndũ wa Kristũ, no inyuĩ mũrĩ andũ oogĩ mũno thĩinĩ wa Kristũ! Ithuĩ nĩ kwaga twagĩte hinya, no inyuĩ mũrĩ na hinya! Inyuĩ nĩmũheetwo gĩtĩĩo, no ithuĩ nĩtũimĩtwo gĩtĩĩo!
English: We are fools for Christ’s sake, but you are wise in Christ. We are weak, but you are strong. You have honor, but we have dishonor.

Kikuyu: Nginya ihinda rĩĩrĩ ithuĩ tũrĩ tũtũũrĩte tũhũũtaga na tũkanyoota, na tũgatarũkĩrwo nĩ nguo, ningĩ nĩtũhũũrĩtwo mũno, o na tũgaikara tũtarĩ na mĩciĩ.
English: Even to this present hour we hunger, thirst, are naked, are beaten, and have no certain dwelling place.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩtũrutaga wĩra na moko maitũ tũrĩ na kĩyo. Rĩrĩa twarumwo-rĩ, ithuĩ tũkarathima; na rĩrĩa twanyariirwo, tũkomĩrĩria;
English: We toil, working with our own hands. When people curse us, we bless. Being persecuted, we endure.

Kikuyu: o na rĩrĩa twacambio, tũcookagia ũhoro na ũhooreri. Nginya ihinda rĩĩrĩ tũtuĩkĩte gĩko gĩa thĩ, tũgatuĩka mahuti ma gũkũ thĩ.
English: Being defamed, we entreat. We are made as the filth of the world, the dirt wiped off by all, even until now.

Kikuyu: Ndiraandĩka marũa maya nĩguo ndĩmũconore, no nĩguo ndĩmũtaare mũrĩ ta ciana ciakwa nyendete mũno.
English: I don’t write these things to shame you, but to admonish you as my beloved children.

Kikuyu: O na mũngĩkorwo mũrĩ na areri ngiri ikũmi thĩinĩ wa Kristũ, mũtirĩ na maithe maingĩ, nĩgũkorwo thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ ndaatuĩkire ithe wanyu ũndũ-inĩ wa Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega.
English: For though you have ten thousand tutors in Christ, you don’t have many fathers. For in Christ Jesus, I became your father through the Good News.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndamũthaitha mwĩgerekanie na niĩ.
English: I beg you therefore, be imitators of me.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa gĩtũmi gĩkĩ nĩngũmũtũmĩra Timotheo, mũrũ wakwa ũrĩa nyendete, na ũrĩa mwĩhokeku thĩinĩ wa Mwathani. Nĩekũmũririkania mũtũũrĩre wakwa thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ, ũrĩa ũringaine na ũrĩa ndutanaga makanitha-inĩ kũndũ guothe.
English: Because of this I have sent Timothy to you, who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, who will remind you of my ways which are in Christ, even as I teach everywhere in every assembly.

Kikuyu: Amwe anyu nĩmatuĩkĩte etĩĩi, makona ta itarooka kũu kũrĩ inyuĩ.
English: Now some are puffed up, as though I were not coming to you.

Kikuyu: No nĩngũũka kũrĩ inyuĩ narua, akorwo nĩ kwenda kwa Mwathani, na hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩngatuĩria ũrĩa andũ aya etĩĩi maaragia, o hamwe na ũhoti ũrĩa marĩ naguo.
English: But I will come to you shortly, if the Lord is willing. And I will know, not the word of those who are puffed up, but the power.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũthamaki wa Ngai ti ũhoro wa kwaria, no nĩ wa kũgĩa na ũhoti.
English: For God’s Kingdom is not in word, but in power.

Kikuyu: Mũkwenda atĩa? Ngĩũke kũrĩ inyuĩ na kĩboko, kana njũke na wendo hamwe na roho wa ũhooreri?
English: What do you want? Shall I come to you with a rod, or in love and a spirit of gentleness?

Kikuyu: Nĩ ma ndeto nĩiraario atĩ thĩinĩ wanyu kũrĩ na ũtharia, na nĩ ũtharia mũthemba ũrĩa ũtangĩoneka o na kũrĩ andũ arĩa matetĩkĩtie Ngai: Naguo nĩ atĩ ũmwe wanyu nĩakomete na mũtumia wa ithe.
English: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you, and such sexual immorality as is not even named among the Gentiles, that one has his father’s wife.

Kikuyu: Na inyuĩ mũgakenera ũndũ ũcio! Githĩ mũtiagĩrĩirwo nĩgũkorwo mũiyũrĩtwo nĩ kĩeha, nĩguo mũndũ ũcio wĩkĩte ũguo mũmũingate, ehere ngwatanĩro-inĩ yanyu?
English: You are arrogant, and didn’t mourn instead, that he who had done this deed might be removed from among you.

Kikuyu: O na ndakorwo ndirĩ hamwe na inyuĩ kũu, ndĩ hamwe na inyuĩ thĩinĩ wa roho, na nĩndĩkĩtie gũtuĩra mũndũ ũcio wĩkĩte ũguo ciira, o ta ndĩ hamwe na inyuĩ.
English: For I most certainly, as being absent in body but present in spirit, have already, as though I were present, judged him who has done this thing.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũrĩkorwo mũnganĩte thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani witũ Jesũ, na niĩ ndĩ hamwe na inyuĩ thĩinĩ wa roho, o na hinya wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ ũrĩ ho-rĩ,
English: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, when you are gathered together with my spirit with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ,

Kikuyu: mũndũ ũcio mũneanei kũrĩ Shaitani, nĩgeetha mehia make ma kĩĩmwĩrĩ maniinwo, naguo roho wake ũkaahonokio mũthenya ũrĩa wa Mwathani.
English: you are to deliver such a one to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.

Kikuyu: Mwĩgaatho ũcio wanyu ti mwega. Kaĩ inyuĩ mũtooĩ atĩ ndawa nini ya kũimbia mĩgate nĩĩtũmaga kĩmere gĩothe kĩimbe?
English: Your boasting is not good. Don’t you know that a little yeast leavens the whole lump?

Kikuyu: Eheriai ndawa ĩyo ya kũimbia mĩgate ĩrĩa ya tene biũ nĩguo mũtuĩke kĩmere kĩerũ gĩtarĩ na ndawa ya kũimbia, tondũ ũguo nĩguo mũtariĩ. Nĩgũkorwo Kristũ, o we gatũrũme gaitũ ka Bathaka, nĩarĩkĩtie kũrutwo igongona.
English: Purge out the old yeast, that you may be a new lump, even as you are unleavened. For indeed Christ, our Passover, has been sacrificed in our place.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, nĩtũkũngũĩre Gĩathĩ, ti na ndawa ĩyo ya tene ya kũimbia mĩgate, ĩyo ya rũmena na ya waganu, no tũkũngũĩre na mũgate ũtarĩ na ndawa ya kũimbia, o guo mũgate wa wĩhokeku na wa ũhoro wa ma.
English: Therefore let’s keep the feast, not with old yeast, neither with the yeast of malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩmwandĩkĩire marũa-inĩ makwa ngamwĩra mũtigage kũgĩaga thiritũ na andũ arĩa ahũũri maraya,
English: I wrote to you in my letter to have no company with sexual sinners;

Kikuyu: ũguo ti kuuga mweheranĩre na andũ a thĩ ĩno, arĩa ahũũri maraya, kana arĩa akoroku, kana arĩa atunyani, o na kana arĩa ahooi mĩhianano. Mũngĩĩka ũguo-rĩ, no mũkiumire gũkũ thĩ.
English: yet not at all meaning with the sexual sinners of this world, or with the covetous and extortionists, or with idolaters, for then you would have to leave the world.

Kikuyu: No rĩu, ndĩramwandĩkĩra nĩguo mũtikagĩe thiritũ na mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ũrĩa wĩĩtaga mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa Ithe witũ, no nĩ mũhũũri maraya, kana nĩ mũkoroku, kana nĩ mũhooi mĩhianano kana nĩ mũndũ wa gũcambania, o na kana nĩ mũrĩĩu wa njoohi kana nĩ mũtunyani. Mũndũ ta ũcio mũtikarĩĩanĩre nake.
English: But as it is, I wrote to you not to associate with anyone who is called a brother who is a sexual sinner, or covetous, or an idolater, or a slanderer, or a drunkard, or an extortionist. Don’t even eat with such a person.

Kikuyu: Ũkĩrĩ wĩra wakwa gũciirithia arĩa matarĩ a kanitha? Githĩ inyuĩ mũtikĩagĩrĩirwo nĩgũciirithia arĩa marĩ a kanitha?
English: For what do I have to do with also judging those who are outside? Don’t you judge those who are within?

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩwe ũgaaciirithia arĩa marĩ nja. “Ingatai mũndũ ũcio mwaganu oime thĩinĩ wanyu.”
English: But those who are outside, God judges. “Put away the wicked man from among yourselves.”*

Kikuyu: Kũngĩkorwo kũrĩ mũndũ ũmwe wanyu ũhĩtĩirie ũrĩa ũngĩ-rĩ, no akĩgerie gũthiĩ kũrĩ andũ arĩa matetigĩrĩte Ngai nĩguo mamatuithanie ciira, handũ ha gũthiĩ mbere ya andũ arĩa aamũre?
English: Dare any of you, having a matter against his neighbor, go to law before the unrighteous, and not before the saints?

Kikuyu: Kaĩ inyuĩ mũtooĩ atĩ andũ arĩa aamũre nĩo magaatuĩra andũ a gũkũ thĩ ciira? Na angĩkorwo nĩ inyuĩ mũgaaciirithia andũ a gũkũ thĩ-rĩ, githĩ mũtikĩrĩ na ũhoti wa gũtua maciira marĩa manini?
English: Don’t you know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is judged by you, are you unworthy to judge the smallest matters?

Kikuyu: Kaĩ inyuĩ mũtooĩ atĩ nĩ ithuĩ tũgaatuĩra araika ciira? Ĩ maũndũ ma mũtũũrĩre ũyũ-rĩ, githĩ tũtiagĩrĩirwo nĩkũmaciira makĩria!
English: Don’t you know that we will judge angels? How much more, things that pertain to this life?

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio kũngĩkorwo na mũndũ wanyu ũhĩtĩirio maũndũ-inĩ ta macio- rĩ, no mũthuure o na andũ matangĩtĩĩka kanitha-inĩ matuĩke a kũmũciirithia!
English: If then you have to judge things pertaining to this life, do you set them to judge who are of no account in the assembly?

Kikuyu: Ndoiga ũguo nĩguo ndĩmũconore. Kwahoteka atĩa atĩ thĩinĩ wanyu gũtirĩ mũndũ mũũgĩ ũngĩhota gũtuithania etĩkia eerĩ ciira mangĩhĩtania?
English: I say this to move you to shame. Isn’t there even one wise man among you who would be able to decide between his brothers?

Kikuyu: No handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa Ithe witũ atwaraga ũrĩa ũngĩ igooti- inĩ, na nĩ igooti-inĩ rĩa arĩa matetĩkĩtie!
English: But brother goes to law with brother, and that before unbelievers!

Kikuyu: Gũkorwo mũrĩ na maciira gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu nĩkuonania atĩ nĩmũhootetwo biũ. Githĩ ti kaba mwĩtĩkagĩre gwĩkwo ũũru? Githĩ ti kaba mwĩtĩkagĩre kũheenio?
English: Therefore it is already altogether a defect in you that you have lawsuits one with another. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be defrauded?

Kikuyu: Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, nĩmũheenanagia na mũgekana ũũru, na acio mwĩkaga ũguo-rĩ, nĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe wanyu.
English: No, but you yourselves do wrong and defraud, and that against your brothers.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ inyuĩ mũtooĩ atĩ andũ arĩa aaganu matikagaya ũthamaki wa Ngai? Mũtikanaheeneke: Gũtirĩ ahũũri maraya, kana ahooi mĩhianano, kana itharia, kana arũme arĩa metuaga ta aka, kana arũme arĩa makomaga na arũme arĩa angĩ,
English: Or don’t you know that the unrighteous will not inherit God’s Kingdom? Don’t be deceived. Neither the sexually immoral, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor male prostitutes, nor homosexuals,

Kikuyu: kana aici, kana andũ arĩa akoroku, kana arĩĩu, kana acambania, o na kana atunyani makaagaya ũthamaki wa Ngai.
English: nor thieves, nor covetous, nor drunkards, nor slanderers, nor extortionists, will inherit God’s Kingdom.

Kikuyu: Na ũguo nĩguo amwe anyu mwatariĩ. No nĩmwathambirio, na mũgĩtherio, na mũgĩtuuo athingu thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ o na nĩ ũndũ wa Roho wa Ngai witũ.
English: Some of you were such, but you were washed. You were sanctified. You were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus, and in the Spirit of our God.

Kikuyu: “Niĩ ndirigĩrĩirio gwĩka ũndũ o wothe,” no ti ũndũ o wothe ũrĩ kĩguni. “Niĩ ndirigĩrĩirio gwĩka ũndũ o wothe,” no ndingĩathwo nĩ ũndũ o na ũrĩkũ.
English: “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are expedient. “All things are lawful for me,” but I will not be brought under the power of anything.

Kikuyu: “Irio nĩ cia nda na nda nĩ ya irio,” no Ngai nĩagaciniina cierĩ. Mwĩrĩ ti wa kũhũthĩrwo na ũmaraya, no nĩ wa gũtungata Mwathani, nake Mwathani aathage mwĩrĩ ũcio.
English: “Foods for the belly, and the belly for foods,” but God will bring to nothing both it and them. But the body is not for sexual immorality, but for the Lord, and the Lord for the body.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩariũkirie Mwathani kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wake, na o na ithuĩ nĩagatũriũkia na hinya o ro ũcio.
English: Now God raised up the Lord, and will also raise us up by his power.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ inyuĩ mũtooĩ atĩ mĩĩrĩ yanyu nĩ ciĩga cia Kristũ we mwene? Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, no ngĩoye ciĩga icio cia Kristũ nĩguo ndĩcinyiitithanie na mũmaraya? Kũroaga gũtuĩka ũguo!
English: Don’t you know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I then take the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute? May it never be!

Kikuyu: Kaĩ inyuĩ mũtooĩ atĩ mũndũ ũrĩa wĩnyiitithanagia na mũmaraya, matuĩkaga mwĩrĩ ũmwe nake? Nĩgũkorwo nĩ kwĩrĩtwo atĩrĩ, “Acio eerĩ magaatuĩka mwĩrĩ ũmwe.”
English: Or don’t you know that he who is joined to a prostitute is one body? For, “The two”, he says, “will become one flesh.”*

Kikuyu: No mũndũ ũrĩa wĩnyiitithanagia na Mwathani atuĩkaga ũmwe nake thĩinĩ wa roho.
English: But he who is joined to the Lord is one spirit.

Kikuyu: Ũragĩrai mehia ma ũmaraya. Mehia marĩa mangĩ mothe mekagwo nĩ mũndũ marĩ nja ya mwĩrĩ wake, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũhũũraga ũmaraya-rĩ, nĩ mwĩrĩ wake mwene ehagĩria.
English: Flee sexual immorality! “Every sin that a man does is outside the body,” but he who commits sexual immorality sins against his own body.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ inyuĩ mũtooĩ atĩ mĩĩrĩ yanyu nĩ hekarũ ya Roho Mũtheru ũrĩa ũrĩ thĩinĩ wanyu, ũrĩa mwaheirwo nĩ Ngai? Inyuĩ mũtirĩ anyu inyuĩ ene;
English: Or don’t you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you, whom you have from God? You are not your own,

Kikuyu: inyuĩ mwagũrirwo na thogora. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, tĩĩithagiai Ngai na mĩĩrĩ yanyu.
English: for you were bought with a price. Therefore glorify God in your body and in your spirit, which are God’s.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ha ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa mwanyandĩkĩire marũa nĩ ũndũ wamo, ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ: Nĩ wega mũndũ mũrũme kwaga kũhikania.
English: Now concerning the things about which you wrote to me: it is good for a man not to touch a woman.

Kikuyu: No tondũ wa ũrĩa ũmaraya ũingĩhĩte-rĩ, o mũndũ mũrũme nĩagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũgĩa na mũtumia wake mwene, na mũndũ-wa-nja agĩe na mũthuuri wake mwene.
English: But, because of sexual immoralities, let each man have his own wife, and let each woman have her own husband.

Kikuyu: Mũthuuri nĩagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũhingagĩria mũtumia wake bata wake wa kĩhiko, o nake mũtumia ahingagĩrie mũthuuriwe bata wake.
English: Let the husband give his wife the affection owed her,† and likewise also the wife her husband.

Kikuyu: Mũtumia ndangĩĩathĩra mwĩrĩ wake mwene, tondũ nĩ wake na mũthuuriwe. Ũguo no taguo mũthuuri atangĩĩathĩra mwĩrĩ wake mwene, tondũ nĩ wake na mũtumia wake.
English: The wife doesn’t have authority over her own body, but the husband does. Likewise also the husband doesn’t have authority over his own body, but the wife does.

Kikuyu: Tigagai kũimana, o tiga rĩrĩa mũngĩkorwo mũiguanĩire, naguo gũkorwo nĩ kwa ihinda, nĩgeetha mũgĩĩage na ihinda rĩa kũhooya. Thuutha ũcio no mũcooke hamwe rĩngĩ, nĩguo Shaitani ndakamũgerie tondũ wanyu kũremwo kwĩgirĩrĩria.
English: Don’t deprive one another, unless it is by consent for a season, that you may give yourselves to fasting and prayer, and may be together again, that Satan doesn’t tempt you because of your lack of self-control.

Kikuyu: Ũhoro ũcio ndawaaria taarĩ rũũtha ngũheana, no ti gwathana ngwathana.
English: But this I say by way of concession, not of commandment.

Kikuyu: No nyende andũ othe mahaane ta ũrĩa niĩ ndariĩ. No o mũndũ aheetwo kĩheo gĩake mwene kuuma kũrĩ Ngai; mũndũ ũmwe aheetwo kĩheo gĩkĩ, na ũrĩa ũngĩ akaheo kĩrĩa kĩngĩ.
English: Yet I wish that all men were like me. However, each man has his own gift from God, one of this kind, and another of that kind.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, nĩ ũndũ wa andũ arĩa matahikanĩtie na atumia a ndigwa, nguuga ũũ: Nĩ wega maikare o ũguo matahikanĩtie, o ta ũrĩa niĩ ndariĩ.
English: But I say to the unmarried and to widows, it is good for them if they remain even as I am.

Kikuyu: No angĩkorwo matingĩhota kwĩgirĩrĩria-rĩ, nĩmahike na mahikanie, tondũ nĩ kaba kũhikania gũkĩra gũikara makĩhĩĩahĩaga.
English: But if they don’t have self-control, let them marry. For it’s better to marry than to burn with passion.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ andũ arĩa mahikĩte na makahikania-rĩ, ngwathana ũũ, no ti niĩ, nĩ Mwathani ũgwathana: Mũtumia ndakanatigane na mũthuuriwe.
English: But to the married I command—not I, but the Lord—that the wife not leave her husband

Kikuyu: No aangĩka ũguo-rĩ, nĩagĩikare atarĩ mũhiku, kana akorwo ti ũguo, acookerere mũthuuriwe. Ningĩ mũthuuri ndakanaingate mũtumia wake.
English: (but if she departs, let her remain unmarried, or else be reconciled to her husband), and that the husband not leave his wife.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa acio angĩ nguuga ũũ, no nĩ niĩ ndĩroiga, ti Mwathani: Angĩkorwo mũrũ wa Ithe witũ arĩ na mũtumia ũtetĩkĩtie, na mũtumia ũcio etĩkĩre matũũranie-rĩ, nĩatige kũmũingata.
English: But to the rest I—not the Lord—say, if any brother has an unbelieving wife, and she is content to live with him, let him not leave her.

Kikuyu: Nake mũtumia angĩkorwo arĩ na mũthuuri ũtetĩkĩtie, na mũthuuri ũcio etĩkĩre matũũranie-rĩ, nĩatige kũmũingata.
English: The woman who has an unbelieving husband, and he is content to live with her, let her not leave her husband.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũthuuri ũrĩa ũtetĩkĩtie nĩatheretio nĩ ũndũ wa mũtumia wake, nake mũtumia ũrĩa ũtarĩ mwĩtĩkia nĩatheretio nĩ ũndũ wa mũthuuri wake mwĩtĩkia. Kũngĩaga gũtuĩka ũguo-rĩ, ciana cianyu no ituĩke ti theru, no ũrĩa kũrĩ rĩu nĩ atĩ nĩ theru.
English: For the unbelieving husband is sanctified in the wife, and the unbelieving wife is sanctified in the husband. Otherwise your children would be unclean, but now they are holy.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũcio ũtetĩkĩtie angĩthiĩ-rĩ, nĩarekwo athiĩ. Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ mũrũme, kana mũndũ-wa-nja ũrĩa wĩtĩkĩtie ti muohe nĩ ũhoro ta ũcio; Ngai atwĩtĩte tũtũũranagie na thayũ.
English: Yet if the unbeliever departs, let there be separation. The brother or the sister is not under bondage in such cases, but God has called us in peace.

Kikuyu: Wee mũtumia-rĩ, ũũĩ atĩa kana nĩwe ũkaahonokia mũthuuri waku? O na wee mũthuuri-rĩ, ũũĩ atĩa kana nĩwe ũkaahonokia mũtumia waku?
English: For how do you know, wife, whether you will save your husband? Or how do you know, husband, whether you will save your wife?

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũndũ o mũndũ nĩaikare mũtũũrĩre ũrĩa Mwathani aamũgaĩire, o ũrĩa Ngai aamwĩtĩire. Ũyũ nĩguo watho ũrĩa ngwatha makanitha mothe.
English: Only, as the Lord has distributed to each man, as God has called each, so let him walk. So I command in all the assemblies.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩkorwo eetirwo arĩ mũruu-rĩ, nĩatige gũikara ta atarĩ mũruu. Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtaarĩ mũruu rĩrĩa eetirwo nĩatige kũrua.
English: Was anyone called having been circumcised? Let him not become uncircumcised. Has anyone been called in uncircumcision? Let him not be circumcised.

Kikuyu: Kũrua gũtirĩ bata; o na kwaga kũrua gũtirĩ bata. Ũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ bata nĩ kũmenyerera maathani ma Ngai.
English: Circumcision is nothing, and uncircumcision is nothing, but what matters is keeping God’s commandments.

Kikuyu: O mũndũ nĩaikarage o ũrĩa aatariĩ rĩrĩa Ngai aamwĩtire.
English: Let each man stay in that calling in which he was called.

Kikuyu: Wee warĩ ngombo rĩrĩa wetirwo? Ũndũ ũcio ndũgagũtange, no korwo wahota kuona njĩra ya kuuma ũkombo-inĩ-rĩ, gerera yo.
English: Were you called being a bondservant? Don’t let that bother you, but if you get an opportunity to become free, use it.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũrĩa warĩ ngombo rĩrĩa Mwathani aamwĩtire-rĩ, ũcio nĩ mũndũ wa Mwathani ũtarĩ ngombo; ningĩ no taguo, ũrĩa warĩ mũndũ muohore rĩrĩa Mwathani aamwĩtire-rĩ, ũcio nĩ ngombo ya Kristũ.
English: For he who was called in the Lord being a bondservant is the Lord’s free man. Likewise he who was called being free is Christ’s bondservant.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ mwagũrirwo goro; mũtigatuĩke ngombo cia andũ.
English: You were bought with a price. Don’t become bondservants of men.

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, o ũndũ ũrĩa mũndũ eehokeirwo nĩ Ngai-rĩ, nĩaikarage o ũguo ũndũ ũcio Ngai aamwĩtĩire.
English: Brothers, let each man, in whatever condition he was called, stay in that condition with God.

Kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, ũhoro wĩgiĩ airĩtu nĩ ũyũ: Ndirĩ na watho uumĩte kũrĩ Mwathani, no nĩngũheana itua rĩakwa ta mũndũ mwĩhokeku nĩ ũndũ wa kũiguĩrwo tha nĩ Mwathani.
English: Now concerning virgins, I have no commandment from the Lord, but I give my judgment as one who has obtained mercy from the Lord to be trustworthy.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa mathĩĩna ma mahinda maya-rĩ, nguona arĩ wega mũikarage o ũrĩa mũndũ atariĩ.
English: Therefore I think that because of the distress that is on us, it’s good for a man to remain as he is.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩũhikanĩtie? Ndũkaingate mũtumia waku. Wee nawe hihi ndũhikanĩtie? Tiga kũhikania.
English: Are you bound to a wife? Don’t seek to be freed. Are you free from a wife? Don’t seek a wife.

Kikuyu: No ũngĩhikania-rĩ, wee ndwĩhĩtie; o na mũirĩtu angĩhika-rĩ, o nake ndehĩtie. No andũ arĩa marahika kana makahikania-rĩ, nĩmarĩonaga mathĩĩna maingĩ mũtũũrĩre-inĩ ũyũ, na nĩmo ndĩrenda kũmũrigĩrĩria.
English: But if you marry, you have not sinned. If a virgin marries, she has not sinned. Yet such will have oppression in the flesh, and I want to spare you.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũrĩa ndĩrenda kuuga, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, nĩ atĩ ihinda nĩ inini. Kuuma rĩu andũ arĩa marĩ na atumia nĩ kwagĩrĩire maikarage ta matarĩ nao;
English: But I say this, brothers: the time is short. From now on, both those who have wives may be as though they had none;

Kikuyu: arĩa maracakaya nĩmaikarage ta mataracakaya; nao arĩa makenete nĩmaikarage ta matakenete; o na arĩa mekũgũra indo, nĩmaikarage ta matarĩ kĩndũ marĩ.
English: and those who weep, as though they didn’t weep; and those who rejoice, as though they didn’t rejoice; and those who buy, as though they didn’t possess;

Kikuyu: nao arĩa mahũthagĩra indo cia thĩ ĩno, nĩmaikarage ta matohanĩtio nacio. Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa thĩ ĩno ĩhaana rĩu-rĩ, nĩgũthira ĩrathira.
English: and those who use the world, as not using it to the fullest. For the mode of this world passes away.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ingĩenda inyuĩ mũikarage mũtegwĩtanga. Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtahikanĩtie etangaga na maũndũ ma Mwathani, na eciiragia o ũrĩa angĩkenia Mwathani.
English: But I desire to have you to be free from cares. He who is unmarried is concerned for the things of the Lord, how he may please the Lord;

Kikuyu: No mũndũ ũrĩa ũhikanĩtie etangaga na maũndũ ma gũkũ thĩ, na eciiragia o ũrĩa angĩkenia mũtumia wake,
English: but he who is married is concerned about the things of the world, how he may please his wife.

Kikuyu: naguo wendi wa ngoro yake nĩmũgayũkanu. Mũndũ-wa-nja ũtarĩ mũhiku kana mũirĩtu etangaga na maũndũ ma Mwathani: Muoroto wake nĩ kwĩheana harĩ Mwathani mwĩrĩ na roho. No mũtumia mũhiku etangaga na maũndũ ma thĩ ĩno, na eciiragia o ũrĩa angĩkenia mũthuuriwe.
English: There is also a difference between a wife and a virgin. The unmarried woman cares about the things of the Lord, that she may be holy both in body and in spirit. But she who is married cares about the things of the world—how she may please her husband.

Kikuyu: Ndĩramwarĩria maũndũ maya nĩgeetha mũgunĩke, ti atĩ nĩguo ndĩmũrigĩrĩrie, no nĩgeetha mũikarage na mĩthiĩre ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire, mwĩheanĩte biũ harĩ Mwathani mũtekũguucĩrĩrio nĩ maũndũ mangĩ.
English: This I say for your own benefit, not that I may ensnare you, but for that which is appropriate, and that you may attend to the Lord without distraction.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe angĩona atĩ nĩarahĩtĩria mũirĩtu ũrĩa orĩtie, nake mũirĩtu ũcio akorwo matukũ make ma ũirĩtu wake nĩmarahĩtũka-rĩ, mũndũ ũcio angĩigua nĩagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũhikania-rĩ, nĩeke o ũguo ekwenda. Ti kwĩhia ekwĩhia. Nĩmagĩrĩirwo mahikanie.
English: But if any man thinks that he is behaving inappropriately toward his virgin, if she is past the flower of her age, and if need so requires, let him do what he desires. He doesn’t sin. Let them marry.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, ũrĩa ũtuĩte na ngoro yake atekũringĩrĩrio, no nĩwe mwene wĩyendeire, na agatua itua atĩ ndekũhikia mũirĩtu ũcio-rĩ, mũndũ ũcioonakenĩekĩteũrĩakwagĩrĩire.
English: But he who stands steadfast in his heart, having no urgency, but has power over his own will, and has determined in his own heart to keep his own virgin, does well.

Kikuyu: Nĩũndũũcio-rĩ, mũndũũrĩaũhikagiamũirĩtuũrĩaorĩtienĩekaga ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire, no ũrĩa ũngĩaga kũmũhikia nĩekaga wega makĩria.
English: So then both he who gives his own virgin in marriage does well, and he who doesn’t give her in marriage does better.

Kikuyu: Mũtumia nĩohanĩtio na mũthuuriwe rĩrĩa rĩothe mũthuuriwe arĩ muoyo. No mũthuuriwe angĩkua, nĩmuohorenanoahikĩremũndũoũrĩaangĩenda, nomũndũũciononginyaakorwonĩetĩkĩtie Mwathani.
English: A wife is bound by law for as long as her husband lives; but if the husband is dead, she is free to be married to whomever she desires, only in the Lord.

Kikuyu: No itua rĩakwa nĩ atĩrĩ, mũtumia ũcio angĩaga kũhika rĩngĩ, no akorwo arĩ na gĩkeno makĩria. Na ngwĩciiria atĩ niĩ o na niĩ ndĩ na Roho wa Ngai.
English: But she is happier if she stays as she is, in my judgment, and I think that I also have God’s Spirit.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ũhoro wa irio iria ikoragwo irutĩirwo mĩhianano-rĩ, nĩtũũĩ atĩ ithuothe tũrĩ na ũmenyo ũhoro-inĩ ũcio. Ũmenyo nĩũtũmaga mũndũ etũũgĩrie, no wendo nĩ gwaka wakaga.
English: Now concerning things sacrificed to idols: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffs up, but love builds up.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wĩciiragia atĩ nĩoĩ ũndũ-rĩ, ndooĩ ũndũ ũcio wega ta ũrĩa aagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũũmenya.
English: But if anyone thinks that he knows anything, he doesn’t yet know as he ought to know.

Kikuyu: No mũndũ ũrĩa wendete Ngai, ũcio nĩwe ũũĩo nĩ Ngai.
English: But anyone who loves God is known by him.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũũcio, haũhoroũkoniĩkũrĩairio iria irutĩirwo mĩhianano-rĩ, nĩtũũĩatĩ gũkũthĩ mũhianano nĩ kĩndũ gĩa tũhũ, na atĩ gũtirĩ Ngai ũngĩ tiga o ũrĩa ũmwe.
English: Therefore concerning the eating of things sacrificed to idols, we know that no idol is anything in the world, and that there is no other God but one.

Kikuyu: Tondũ o na kũngĩkorwo nĩ kũrĩ indo ciĩtagwo ngai, irĩ kũũrĩa igũrũ kana irĩ gũkũ thĩ-rĩ, amu ti-itherũ nĩ kũrĩ na “ngai” nyingĩ na “aathani” aingĩ,
English: For though there are things that are called “gods”, whether in the heavens or on earth—as there are many “gods” and many “lords”—

Kikuyu: no harĩ ithuĩ-rĩ, Ngai no ũmwe, na nĩwe Ithe witũ, na indo ciothe cioimire kũrĩ we na tũtũũraga muoyo nĩ ũndũ wake; na kũrĩ na Mwathani o ũmwe, nake nĩwe Jesũ Kristũ, o we ũrĩa indo ciothe ciagĩire nĩ ũndũ wake, na nowe ũtũtũũragia muoyo.
English: yet to us there is one God, the Father, of whom are all things, and we for him; and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom are all things, and we live through him.

Kikuyu: No ti andũ othe mooĩ ũguo. Andũ amwe nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa matũũrĩte mamenyerete mĩhianano, o na hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mekũrĩa irio ta icio meciiragia atĩ nĩirutĩirwo mũhianano, na tondũ thamiri ciao itirĩ hinya-rĩ, makanyiitwo nĩ thaahu.
English: However, that knowledge isn’t in all men. But some, with consciousness of an idol until now, eat as of a thing sacrificed to an idol, and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.

Kikuyu: No irio iria tũrĩĩaga itingĩtũma tũkuhĩrĩrie Ngai; tũtingĩthũkio nĩ kwaga gũcirĩa, na tũtingĩagĩrio nĩgũcirĩa.
English: But food will not commend us to God. For neither, if we don’t eat are we the worse, nor if we eat are we the better.

Kikuyu: O na kũrĩ ũguo-rĩ, menyererai atĩ wĩyathi ũcio wanyu ndũgatuĩke mũhĩnga kũrĩ arĩa matarĩ na hinya.
English: But be careful that by no means does this liberty of yours become a stumbling block to the weak.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ o wothe ũrĩ na thamiri ĩtarĩ na hinya, angĩmuona inyuĩ mũrĩ na ũmenyo ũyũ mũkĩrĩĩra irio thĩinĩ wa hekarũ ya mũhianano-rĩ, githĩ ndangĩũmĩrĩria arĩe kĩrĩa kĩrutĩirwo mĩhianano?
English: For if a man sees you who have knowledge sitting in an idol’s temple, won’t his conscience, if he is weak, be emboldened to eat things sacrificed to idols?

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa Ithe witũ ũcio thamiri yake ĩtarĩ na hinya, o ũcio Kristũ aakuĩrĩire-rĩ, agathũkio nĩ ũmenyo wanyu.
English: And through your knowledge, he who is weak perishes, the brother for whose sake Christ died.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mwehĩria ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ na njĩra ĩyo, na mũgatiihia thamiri ciao icio itarĩ hinya-rĩ, nĩ Kristũ mwehĩria.
English: Thus, sinning against the brothers, and wounding their conscience when it is weak, you sin against Christ.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, angĩkorwo kĩrĩa ndĩrarĩa no gĩtũme mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa Ithe witũ agwe mehia-inĩ-rĩ, ndikĩagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũrĩa nyama rĩngĩ, nĩguo ndigatũme agwe.
English: Therefore, if food causes my brother to stumble, I will eat no meat forever more, that I don’t cause my brother to stumble.

Kikuyu: Githĩ niĩ ndirĩ na wĩyathi? Githĩ niĩ ndirĩ mũtũmwo? Githĩ niĩ ndionire Jesũ Mwathani witũ? Anga inyuĩ mũtirĩ maciaro ma wĩra wakwa thĩinĩ wa Mwathani?
English: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Haven’t I seen Jesus Christ, our Lord? Aren’t you my work in the Lord?

Kikuyu: O na angĩkorwo andũ arĩa angĩ mationaga ta ndĩ mũtũmwo-rĩ, ti-itherũ niĩ harĩ inyuĩ ndĩ mũtũmwo! Nĩgũkorwo nĩ inyuĩ kĩmenyithia kĩa ũtũmwo wakwa thĩinĩ wa Mwathani.
English: If to others I am not an apostle, yet at least I am to you; for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.

Kikuyu: Gwĩtetera gwakwa harĩ arĩa manjiirithagia nĩ gũkũ:
English: My defense to those who examine me is this:

Kikuyu: Githĩ ithuĩ tũtirĩ na kĩhooto gĩa kũrĩa na kũnyua?
English: Have we no right to eat and to drink?

Kikuyu: Githĩ ithuĩ tũtirĩ na kĩhooto kĩa mũndũ gũtwarana rũgendo-inĩ na mũtumia wake mwĩtĩkia, o ta ũrĩa atũmwo arĩa angĩ, na ariũ a nyina na Mwathani, o na Kefa mekaga?
English: Have we no right to take along a wife who is a believer, even as the rest of the apostles, and the brothers of the Lord, and Cephas?

Kikuyu: Kana no niĩ tũrĩ na Baranaba arĩ o nginya tũrutage wĩra nĩguo tuone ũteithio witũ?
English: Or have only Barnabas and I no right to not work?

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũtungataga ta mũthigari na akeerĩha na indo ciake we mwene? Nĩ mũndũ ũrĩkũ ũhaandaga mũgũnda wa mĩthabibũ na ndarĩe thabibũ cia mũgũnda ũcio? Ningĩ-rĩ, nĩ kũrĩ mũndũ ũrĩithagia rũũru na ndanyue iria rĩa rũũru rũu?
English: What soldier ever serves at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard, and doesn’t eat of its fruit? Or who feeds a flock, and doesn’t drink from the flock’s milk?

Kikuyu: Ndĩraaria ũhoro ũyũ ndĩ na meciiria ma ũ-mũndũ tu? Githĩ Watho o naguo nduugĩte o ũguo?
English: Do I speak these things according to the ways of men? Or doesn’t the law also say the same thing?

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩ kwandĩkĩtwo watho-inĩ wa Musa atĩrĩ, “Ndũkanohe ndegwa kanua hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ĩkũranga ngano ĩkĩmĩhũũra.” Ngai akiuga ũguo-rĩ, nĩ ndegwa aracaaĩra?
English: For it is written in the law of Moses, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it treads out the grain.”* Is it for the oxen that God cares,

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ oigaga ũguo nĩ ũndũ witũ, githĩ tiguo? Ĩĩ-ni, ũndũ ũyũ waandĩkirũo nĩ ũndũ witũ, tondũ rĩrĩa mũrĩmi ekũrĩma, nake mũhũũri ngano amĩhũũrage-rĩ, nĩ kwagĩrĩire mekage ũguo marĩ na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro atĩ nĩmakagayana magetha.
English: or does he say it assuredly for our sake? Yes, it was written for our sake, because he who plows ought to plow in hope, and he who threshes in hope should partake of his hope.

Kikuyu: Tũngĩkorwo ithuĩ nĩtũhaandĩte mbeũ ya kĩĩroho thĩinĩ wanyu-rĩ, kaĩ akĩrĩ ũndũ mũnene ithuĩ tũngĩgetha indo cia gũtũũria mwĩrĩ kuuma kũrĩ inyuĩ?
English: If we sowed to you spiritual things, is it a great thing if we reap your fleshly things?

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo andũ angĩ marĩ na kĩhooto gĩa gũteithio nĩ inyuĩ-rĩ, ithuĩ githĩ tũtikĩagĩrĩirwo nĩgũteithio makĩria? No ithuĩ tũtiahũthĩrire kĩhooto kĩu. Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, nĩ gũkirĩrĩria tũkiragĩrĩria ũndũ o wothe nĩguo tũtikae kũhĩngĩca Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa Kristũ.
English: If others partake of this right over you, don’t we yet more? Nevertheless we didn’t use this right, but we bear all things, that we may cause no hindrance to the Good News of Christ.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ inyuĩ mũtooĩ atĩ andũ arĩa marutaga wĩra thĩinĩ wa hekarũ marutaga irio ciao kuuma kũu hekarũ, nao arĩa matungataga kĩgongona-inĩ magayanaga kĩrĩa kĩrutĩtwo kĩgongona-inĩ?
English: Don’t you know that those who serve around sacred things eat from the things of the temple, and those who wait on the altar have their portion with the altar?

Kikuyu: O ta ũguo-rĩ, Mwathani nĩathanĩte atĩ arĩa mahunjagia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega nĩmagĩrĩirwo kuonaga gĩa kũmatũũria kuuma kũrĩ Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega.
English: Even so the Lord ordained that those who proclaim the Good News should live from the Good News.

Kikuyu: No niĩ ndirĩ ndaahũthĩra kĩhooto o na kĩmwe gĩa cio. Ningĩ ndiraandĩka ũhoro ũyũ ndĩ na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro atĩ nĩmũkũnjĩka maũndũ ta macio. Nĩ kaba ngue gũkĩra mũndũ o naũ agirie ndĩĩrahĩre ũndũ ũcio.
English: But I have used none of these things, and I don’t write these things that it may be done so in my case; for I would rather die, than that anyone should make my boasting void.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, ndingĩhota kwĩraha rĩrĩa ngũhunjia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega, nĩgũkorwo nĩhatagĩrĩrio mũno ndĩũhunjie. Na niĩ ingĩaga kũhunjia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega kaĩ ndagĩa na haaro-ĩ!
English: For if I preach the Good News, I have nothing to boast about, for necessity is laid on me; but woe is to me if I don’t preach the Good News.

Kikuyu: Ingĩhunjia nĩ ũndũ wa kwĩyendera-rĩ, ndaheo mũcaara; no angĩkorwo ti na ũndũ wa kwĩyendera-rĩ, niĩ no kũhingia ndĩrahingia ũndũ ũrĩa ndĩhokeirwo.
English: For if I do this of my own will, I have a reward. But if not of my own will, I have a stewardship entrusted to me.

Kikuyu: Hakĩrĩ ũguo-rĩ, mũcaara wakwa ũkĩrĩ kĩ? Nĩ atĩrĩ: Atĩ harĩ ũhoro wa kũhunjia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega-rĩ, hunjagie tũhũ itekũrĩhwo, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio njage gwĩtagia kĩhooto gĩakwa rĩrĩa ngũhunjia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega.
English: What then is my reward? That when I preach the Good News, I may present the Good News of Christ without charge, so as not to abuse my authority in the Good News.

Kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka ndĩ muohore na ndirĩ ngombo ya mũndũ-rĩ, niĩ ndĩĩtuaga ngombo ya o mũndũ wothe, nĩguo nguucĩrĩrie andũ aingĩ o ta ũrĩa kwahoteka.
English: For though I was free from all, I brought myself under bondage to all, that I might gain the more.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ Ayahudi ndaatuĩkire ta Mũyahudi nĩguo nguucagĩrĩrie Ayahudi. Na kũrĩ arĩa marĩ rungu rwa watho-rĩ, ndaatuĩkire ta mũndũ ũrĩ rungu rwa watho, o na gũtuĩka niĩ mwene ndirĩ rungu rwa watho, nĩguo nguucagĩrĩrie arĩa marĩ rungu rwa watho.
English: To the Jews I became as a Jew, that I might gain Jews; to those who are under the law, as under the law,† that I might gain those who are under the law;

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ arĩa matarĩ watho-inĩ-rĩ, ndaatuĩkire ta mũndũ ũtarĩ watho-inĩ ũcio, o na gũtuĩka ndirĩ muohore kuuma kũrĩ watho wa Ngai, no ndĩ watho-inĩ wa Kristũ, nĩguo nguucagĩrĩrie arĩa matarĩ watho-inĩ ũcio.
English: to those who are without law, as without law (not being without law toward God, but under law toward Christ), that I might win those who are without law.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ arĩa matarĩ hinya-rĩ, ndaatuĩkire ta itarĩ hinya nĩguo nguucagĩrĩrie arĩa matarĩ hinya. Nduĩkĩte maũndũ mothe kũrĩ andũ othe nĩgeetha kũngĩhoteka na njĩra ciothe ngerie kũhonokia amwe ao.
English: To the weak I became as weak, that I might gain the weak. I have become all things to all men, that I may by all means save some.

Kikuyu: Njĩkaga maũndũ maya mothe nĩ ũndũ wa Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega, nĩgeetha ngaagaĩrwo irathimo ciaguo.
English: Now I do this for the sake of the Good News, that I may be a joint partaker of it.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ inyuĩ mũtooĩ atĩ andũ arĩa mahĩtahĩtanaga ihenya-inĩ mahanyũkaga othe, no rĩrĩ, no ũmwe wao ũheagwo kĩheo? Kĩhanyũkei na njĩra ĩrĩa ĩngĩtũma mũkaaheo kĩheo.*
English: Don’t you know that those who run in a race all run, but one receives the prize? Run like that, so that you may win.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũhĩtahĩtanaga thĩinĩ wa mathako-rĩ, ehataga na akemenyeria maũndũ-inĩ mothe hatarĩ itherũ. Mekaga ũguo nĩguo maheo thũmbĩ ĩtarĩ ya gũtũũra; no ithuĩ twĩkaga ũguo nĩguo tũkaaheo thũmbĩ ya gũtũũra nginya tene.
English: Every man who strives in the games exercises self-control in all things. Now they do it to receive a corruptible crown, but we an incorruptible.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, niĩ ndihanyũkaga ta mũndũ ũtooĩ gĩtũmi gĩa kũhanyũka; na ndirũaga ta mũndũ ũgũikia ngundi ciake o ũguo tũhũ.
English: I therefore run like that, not aimlessly. I fight like that, not beating the air,

Kikuyu: Aca, nĩ mwĩrĩ wakwa hũũraga ngaũtua ngombo yakwa, nĩgeetha ndaarĩkia kũhunjĩria andũ arĩa angĩ-rĩ, niĩ mwene ndikanatuĩke wa kũregwo nyimwo kĩheo.
English: but I beat my body and bring it into submission, lest by any means, after I have preached to others, I myself should be disqualified.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, ndikwenda mũkorwo mũtooĩ ũndũ ũyũ, atĩ maithe maitũ ma tene othe maatongoragio nĩ itu, na atĩ othe nĩmaringire iria.
English: Now I would not have you ignorant, brothers, that our fathers were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea;

Kikuyu: Othe nĩmabatithirio matuĩke andũ a Musa marĩ rungu rwa itu rĩu, o na marĩ iria-inĩ rĩu.
English: and were all baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea;

Kikuyu: Na othe maarĩĩaga irio o imwe cia kĩĩroho,
English: and all ate the same spiritual food;

Kikuyu: na maanyuuaga maaĩ o mamwe ma kĩĩroho; nĩgũkorwo maanyuire kuuma rwaro-inĩ rwa ihiga rwa kĩĩroho rũrĩa rwatwaranaga nao, naruo rwaro rũu rwarĩ Kristũ.
English: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank of a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.

Kikuyu: O na kũrĩ ũguo-rĩ, aingĩ ao matiigana gũkenia Ngai; nacio ciimba ciao ikĩhurunjwo werũ-inĩ.
English: However with most of them, God was not well pleased, for they were overthrown in the wilderness.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, maũndũ macio meekĩkire marĩ ta cionereria, nĩgeetha ngoro ciitũ itikerirĩrie gwĩka maũndũ mooru ta macio meekire.
English: Now these things were our examples, to the intent we should not lust after evil things as they also lusted.

Kikuyu: Mũtigatuĩke ahooi mĩhianano, ta ũrĩa amwe ao maatariĩ; o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Andũ acio magĩikara thĩ kũrĩa na kũnyua, magĩcooka magĩũkĩra kũina na gwĩkenia.”
English: Don’t be idolaters, as some of them were. As it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.”*

Kikuyu: Ningĩ tũtikahũũre ũmaraya, ta ũrĩa amwe ao meekire, nao magĩkua andũ ngiri mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na ithatũ mũthenya ũmwe.
English: Let’s not commit sexual immorality, as some of them committed, and in one day twenty-three thousand fell.

Kikuyu: O na ningĩ tũtikanagerie Mwathani, ta ũrĩa amwe ao meekire, nao makĩniinwo nĩ nyoka.
English: Let’s not test Christ,† as some of them tested, and perished by the serpents.

Kikuyu: Na mũtikae gũteta, ta ũrĩa amwe ao meekire, nao makĩũragwo nĩ mũraika ũrĩa mũniinani.
English: Don’t grumble, as some of them also grumbled, and perished by the destroyer.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ macio meekĩkire kũrĩ o marĩ cionereria, na makĩandĩkwo marĩ ma gũtũkaania, ithuĩ tũkinyĩrĩirwo nĩ kũhingio kwa mahinda.
English: Now all these things happened to them by way of example, and they were written for our admonition, on whom the ends of the ages have come.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ũngĩĩra nĩũrũgamĩte wega-rĩ, menyerera ndũkae kũgũa!
English: Therefore let him who thinks he stands be careful that he doesn’t fall.

Kikuyu: Mũtirĩ mwagerio na magerio mangĩ tiga o marĩa makoraga andũ. Nake Ngai nĩmwĩhokeku; ndangĩtĩkĩra mũgerio na magerio marĩa mũtangĩhota gwĩtiiria. No rĩrĩa mũkũgerio-rĩ, nĩarĩmuonagĩrĩria njĩra ya kũũrĩra nĩguo mũhotage gwĩtiiria magerio macio.
English: No temptation has taken you except what is common to man. God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tempted above what you are able, but will with the temptation also make the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, arata akwa endwa, ũragĩrai ũhoro wa kũhooya mĩhianano.
English: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.

Kikuyu: Ndĩraarĩria andũ oogĩ; mwĩkũũranĩrei ũrĩa ndĩroiga.
English: I speak as to wise men. Judge what I say.

Kikuyu: Gĩkombe gĩa gũcookia ngaatho kĩrĩa tũcookagia ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wakĩo-rĩ, githĩ ti kũgwatanĩra thakame ya Kristũ?* Na githĩ mũgate ũrĩa twenyũraga ti kũgwatanĩra mwĩrĩ wa Kristũ?
English: The cup of blessing which we bless, isn’t it a sharing of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, isn’t it a sharing of the body of Christ?

Kikuyu: Na tondũ mũgate ũcio nĩ ũmwe-rĩ, ithuĩ, o na tũrĩ aingĩ, tũrĩ o mwĩrĩ ũmwe, nĩgũkorwo ithuĩ othe tũrĩĩaga mũgate o ũcio ũmwe.
English: Because there is one loaf of bread, we, who are many, are one body; for we all partake of the one loaf of bread.

Kikuyu: Ta mwĩcũraniei ũhoro wa andũ a Isiraeli: Arĩa marĩĩaga irio cia igongona-rĩ, githĩ matig- watanagĩra kĩgongona kĩu?
English: Consider Israel according to the flesh. Don’t those who eat the sacrifices participate in the altar?

Kikuyu: Ndĩrenda kuuga atĩa? Atĩ kĩrĩa kĩrutĩirwo mũhianano nĩ kĩndũ kũrĩ, kana atĩ mũhianano guo mwene nĩ kĩndũ kũrĩ?
English: What am I saying then? That a thing sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?

Kikuyu: Aca! Ũrĩa ndĩroiga nĩ atĩ magongona ma andũ arĩa matarĩ na ũngai marutagĩrwo ndaimono, na ti Ngai, na ndikwenda mũgĩe ngwatanĩro na ndaimono.
English: But I say that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons and not to God, and I don’t desire that you would have fellowship with demons.

Kikuyu: Mũtingĩhota kũnyuĩra gĩkombe kĩa Mwathani, na mũnyuĩre gĩkombe kĩa ndaimono o nakĩo; mũtingĩhota kũgwatanĩra metha-inĩ ya Mwathani, na mũgwatanĩre metha-inĩ ya ndaimono.
English: You can’t both drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You can’t both partake of the table of the Lord and of the table of demons.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ tũrageria kwarahũra ũiru wa Mwathani? Kaĩ tũrĩ na hinya kũmũkĩra?
English: Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?

Kikuyu: “Niĩ ndirigĩrĩirio gwĩka ũndũ,” no ti ũndũ o wothe ũrĩ na kĩguni. “Niĩ ndirigĩrĩirio gwĩka ũndũ,” no ti ũndũ o wothe ũrĩ na uumithio.
English: “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are profitable. “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things build up.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ wagĩrĩirwo nĩgũcaragia maũndũ ma kwĩguna we mwene, no nĩacaragie maũndũ ma kũguna andũ arĩa angĩ.
English: Let no one seek his own, but each one his neighbor’s good.

Kikuyu: Rĩĩagai kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩendagio thoko-inĩ ya nyama mũtekwĩyũria ciũria nĩ ũndũ wa mwĩcuuko wa thamiri,
English: Whatever is sold in the butcher shop, eat, asking no question for the sake of conscience,

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo, “Thĩ nĩ ya Mwathani, na kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩrĩ thĩinĩ wayo.”
English: for “the earth is the Lord’s, and its fullness.”*

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũtarĩ mwĩtĩkia angĩmwĩta mũkarĩe irio gwake, na inyuĩ mũkorwo nĩ mũkwenda gũthiĩ-rĩ, rĩa irio ciothe iria mũngĩheo mũtekwĩyũria ciũria cia mwĩcuuko wa thamiri.
English: But if one of those who don’t believe invites you to a meal, and you are inclined to go, eat whatever is set before you, asking no questions for the sake of conscience.

Kikuyu: No mũndũ angĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Irio ici irutĩtwo irĩ igongona,” mũtigacirĩe, nĩ ũndũ wa mũndũ ũcio wamwĩra ũguo, o na nĩ ũndũ wa mwĩcuuko wa thamiri;
English: But if anyone says to you, “This was offered to idols,” don’t eat it for the sake of the one who told you, and for the sake of conscience. For “the earth is the Lord’s, with all its fullness.”

Kikuyu: ngiuga ũguo ti thamiri yaku ndagweta, no nĩ thamiri ya mũndũ ũcio ũngĩ. Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, wĩyathi wakwa ũgũciirithio nĩkĩ nĩ ũndũ wa thamiri ya mũndũ ũngĩ?
English: Conscience, I say, not your own, but the other’s conscience. For why is my liberty judged by another conscience?

Kikuyu: Ingĩrĩanĩra irio na andũ angĩ ndĩ na ngaatho-rĩ, nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma njambio nĩ ũndũ wa kĩndũ kĩu njokeirie Ngai ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wakĩo?
English: If I partake with thankfulness, why am I denounced for something I give thanks for?

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, rĩrĩa mũkũrĩa kana rĩrĩa mũkũnyua, o na kana mũgĩĩka ũndũ o wothe-rĩ, wĩkagei nĩ ũndũ wa kwenda kũgoocithia Ngai.
English: Whether therefore you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.

Kikuyu: Tigai gwĩka ũndũ ũngĩhĩngithia mũndũ, arĩ Mũyahudi, kana arĩ Mũyunani, o na kana ũhĩngithie kanitha wa Ngai,
English: Give no occasion for stumbling, whether to Jews, to Greeks, or to the assembly of God;

Kikuyu: o ta ũrĩa niĩ ngeragia gũkenia andũ othe na njĩra o yothe. Nĩgũkorwo ndicaragia ũndũ wa kwĩguna niĩ mwene, no njaragia ũrĩa ingĩguna andũ aingĩ, nĩguo mahonoke.
English: even as I also please all men in all things, not seeking my own profit, but the profit of the many, that they may be saved.

Kikuyu: Mwĩgerekaniei na niĩ, ta ũrĩa ndĩgerekanagia na Kristũ.
English: Be imitators of me, even as I also am of Christ.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũmũgaathĩrĩria nĩ ũndũ wa kũndirikana maũndũ-inĩ mothe, na nĩ ũndũ wa gũtũũra mũrũmĩtie morutani macio, o ta ũrĩa ndaamũrutire.
English: Now I praise you, brothers, that you remember me in all things, and hold firm the traditions, even as I delivered them to you.

Kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, nĩngwenda mũmenye atĩ mũtwe wa mũndũ mũrũme o wothe nĩ Kristũ, na mũtwe wa mũndũ-wa-nja nĩ mũndũ mũrũme, na mũtwe wa Kristũ nĩ Ngai.
English: But I would have you know that the head† of every man is Christ, and the head‡ of the woman is man, and the head§ of Christ is God.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũrũme o wothe ũrĩa ũhooyaga kana akaratha ehumbĩrĩte mũtwe-rĩ, nĩ mũtwe wake aconorithagia.
English: Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonors his head.

Kikuyu: Nake mũndũ-wa-nja o wothe ũrĩa ũhooyaga kana akaratha atahumbĩrĩte mũtwe wake-rĩ, nĩ mũtwe wake aconorithagia, tondũ ũguo no ũndũ ũmwe na kwenjwo.
English: But every woman praying or prophesying with her head uncovered dishonors her head. For it is one and the same thing as if she were shaved.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo mũndũ-wa-nja ndekũhumbĩra mũtwe wake- rĩ, nĩakĩenje njuĩrĩ yake; na angĩkorwo kũrengarenga njuĩrĩ kana kwenjwo nĩ ũndũ wa gũconora mũndũ-wa-nja-rĩ, nĩakĩĩhumbĩre mũtwe.
English: For if a woman is not covered, let her hair also be cut off. But if it is shameful for a woman to have her hair cut off or be shaved, let her be covered.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũrũme ndaagĩrĩirwo nĩkũhumbĩra mũtwe wake, kuona atĩ nĩwe mũhianĩre wa Ngai, na nĩ riiri wa Ngai; no mũndũ-wa-nja nĩwe riiri wa mũndũ mũrũme.
English: For a man indeed ought not to have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God, but the woman is the glory of the man.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ mũrũme ndoimire harĩ mũndũ-wa-nja, no nĩ mũndũ-wa-nja woimire harĩ mũndũ mũrũme.
English: For man is not from woman, but woman from man;

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũrũme-rĩ, tiwe wombirwo nĩ ũndũ wa mũndũ-wa-nja, no nĩ mũndũ- wa-nja wombirwo nĩ ũndũ wa mũndũ mũrũme.
English: for man wasn’t created for the woman, but woman for the man.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa gĩtũmi gĩkĩ-rĩ, mũndũ-wa-nja agĩrĩirwo nĩgũkorwo na rũũri mũtwe rwa kuonania atĩ nĩ wa gwathwo nĩ ũndũ wa araika.
English: For this cause the woman ought to have authority over her own head, because of the angels.

Kikuyu: No ningĩ-rĩ, thĩinĩ wa Mwathani, mũndũ-wa-nja ndeiganĩtie mũndũ mũrũme atarĩ ho, nake mũndũ-mũrũme ndeiganĩtie mũndũ-wa-nja atarĩ ho.
English: Nevertheless, neither is the woman independent of the man, nor the man independent of the woman, in the Lord.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa mũndũ-wa-nja oimire harĩ mũndũ mũrũme-rĩ, no taguo mũndũ-mũrũme aciaragwo nĩ mũndũ-wa-nja. No rĩrĩ, indo ciothe ciumaga kũrĩ Ngai.
English: For as woman came from man, so a man also comes through a woman; but all things are from God.

Kikuyu: Ta tuai arĩ inyuĩ: Nĩ kwagĩrĩire mũndũ-wa-nja kũhooya Ngai atehumbĩrĩte mũtwe?
English: Judge for yourselves. Is it appropriate that a woman pray to God unveiled?

Kikuyu: Kaĩ maũndũ ma ndũire matamũrutaga atĩ mũndũ-mũrũme gũkorwo arĩ na njuĩrĩ ndaaya nĩ ũndũ wa kũmũconorithia,
English: Doesn’t even nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a dishonor to him?

Kikuyu: no mũndũ-wa-nja gũkorwo na njuĩrĩ ndaaya, ũcio nĩ riiri wake? Nĩgũkorwo aheetwo njuĩrĩ ndaaya arĩ kĩndũ gĩa kũmũhumbĩra.
English: But if a woman has long hair, it is a glory to her, for her hair is given to her for a covering.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo kũrĩ na mũndũ ũrĩ na ngarari na ũhoro ũyũ-rĩ, nĩamenye atĩ ithuĩ tũtirĩ mũtugo ũngĩ, o na makanitha ma Ngai matirĩ mũtugo ũngĩ.
English: But if any man seems to be contentious, we have no such custom, neither do God’s assemblies.

Kikuyu: Ũhoro-inĩ ũrĩa ngũmũtaarĩria rĩu-rĩ, ndirĩ na ũndũ wa kũmũgaathĩra, nĩgũkorwo kũngana kwanyu ti gwa kũmũguna no nĩ gwa kũmũthũkia.
English: But in giving you this command I don’t praise you, because you come together not for the better but for the worse.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ wa mbere, njiguaga atĩ rĩrĩa muongana ta kanitha, nĩkũgĩaga na nyamũkano gatagatĩ kanyu, na ũhoro ũcio ndingĩaga gwĩtĩkia ũmwe waguo.
English: For first of all, when you come together in the assembly, I hear that divisions exist among you, and I partly believe it.

Kikuyu: Hatarĩ nganja gũtingĩaga kũgĩa na ngarari gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu cia kuonania arĩa anyu metĩkĩrĩkĩte nĩ Ngai.
English: For there also must be factions among you, that those who are approved may be revealed among you.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa muongana hamwe, ti Irio cia Mwathani mũrĩĩaga,
English: When therefore you assemble yourselves together, it is not the Lord’s supper that you eat.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo rĩrĩa mũkũrĩa, o mũndũ athiiaga na mbere kũrĩa ategweterera ũrĩa ũngĩ. Ũmwe aikaraga ahũtiĩ, nake ũrĩa ũngĩ agaikara arĩĩtwo.
English: For in your eating each one takes his own supper first. One is hungry, and another is drunken.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ mũtarĩ mĩciĩ mũngĩrĩĩra na mũnyuĩre kuo? Kana nĩ kanitha wa Ngai mũnyararĩte na mũkenda gũconoraga arĩa matarĩ kĩndũ? Ngũkĩmwĩra atĩa? No ndĩkĩmũgaathĩrĩrie nĩ ũndũ wa ũhoro ũcio? Aca, ndingĩmũgaathĩrĩria!
English: What, don’t you have houses to eat and to drink in? Or do you despise God’s assembly and put them to shame who don’t have enough? What shall I tell you? Shall I praise you? In this I don’t praise you.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũhoro ũrĩa ndaamũkĩrire kuuma kũrĩ Mwathani noguo ndaamũkinyĩirie: Mwathani Jesũ, ũtukũ ũrĩa aakunyanĩirwo, nĩooire mũgate,
English: For I received from the Lord that which also I delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night in which he was betrayed took bread.

Kikuyu: na aarĩkia gũcookia ngaatho,* akĩwenyũranga, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩ mwĩrĩ wakwa, ũrĩa ũheanĩtwo nĩ ũndũ wanyu; ĩkagai ũũ nĩguo mũndirikanage.”
English: When he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “Take, eat. This is my body, which is broken for you. Do this in memory of me.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ o ũndũ ũmwe, thuutha wa kũrĩa irio, akĩoya gĩkombe, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Gĩkombe gĩkĩ nĩ kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa kĩerũ thĩinĩ wa thakame yakwa; ĩkagai ũũ, rĩrĩa rĩothe mũrĩkĩnyuuagĩra, nĩguo mũndirikanage.”
English: In the same way he also took the cup after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink, in memory of me.”

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo rĩrĩa rĩothe mũkũrĩa mũgate ũyũ na mũkanyuĩra gĩkombe gĩkĩ-rĩ, nĩkuumbũra mũkuumbũra gũkua kwa Mwathani o nginya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa agooka.
English: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, mũndũ o wothe ũkũrĩa mũgate ũcio kana akanyuĩra gĩkombe kĩu kĩa Mwathani ũrĩa gũtaagĩrĩire-rĩ, nĩkwĩhiaehĩiriemwĩrĩnathakameyaMwathani.
English: Therefore whoever eats this bread or drinks the Lord’s cup in a way unworthy of the Lord will be guilty of the body and the blood of the Lord.

Kikuyu: Mũndũnĩambagegwĩtuĩriambere ya kũrĩa mũgate ũcio na kũnyuĩra gĩkombe kĩu.
English: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of the bread and drink of the cup.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũrĩĩaga na akanyua ategũkũũranamwĩrĩwaMwathani, nĩituĩrorĩakewemweneetuagĩra.
English: For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy way eats and drinks judgment to himself if he doesn’t discern the Lord’s body.

Kikuyu: Kĩunĩkĩogĩtũmĩteaingĩanyu moorwo nĩ hinya, na marũare, na amwe anyu nĩmarĩkĩtie gũkua.
English: For this cause many among you are weak and sickly, and not a few sleep.

Kikuyu: No tũngĩĩtuĩra ciira ithuĩ ene, tũtingĩtuĩka a gũciirithio.
English: For if we discerned ourselves, we wouldn’t be judged.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa tũgũtuĩrwo ciira nĩ Mwathani-rĩ, nĩkũherithio tũherithagio nĩgeetha tũtikae gũtuĩrwo ciira hamwe na thĩ.
English: But when we are judged, we are disciplined by the Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, rĩrĩa mũnganĩte mũrĩĩanĩre-rĩ, etanagĩrĩrai.
English: Therefore, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo mũndũ nĩahũũtiĩ-rĩ, agĩrĩirwo nĩ kũrĩa irio gwake mũciĩ, nĩgeetha rĩrĩa muongana gũtikagĩe ũhoro wa gũtuĩrwo ciira. Nĩngaheana ũhoro wa maũndũ macio mangĩ rĩrĩa ngooka.
English: But if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, lest your coming together be for judgment. The rest I will set in order whenever I come.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, ha ũhoro wa iheo cia kĩĩroho-rĩ, ndikwenda mũikare mũrigĩtwo nĩguo.
English: Now concerning spiritual things, brothers, I don’t want you to be ignorant.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ nĩmũũĩ atĩ hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũteetĩkĩtie Ngai, nĩmwaguucagĩrĩrio na mũkerekerio kũrĩ mĩhianano ĩtangĩaria.
English: You know that when you were heathen,† you were led away to those mute idols, however you might be led.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngũmwĩra atĩ, gũtirĩ mũndũ waragia ahotithĩtio nĩ Roho wa Ngai ũngiuga atĩrĩ, “Jesũ aronyiitwo nĩ kĩrumi,” na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩhota kuuga atĩrĩ, “Jesũ nĩ Mwathani,” atatongoretio nĩ Roho Mũtheru.
English: Therefore I make known to you that no man speaking by God’s Spirit says, “Jesus is accursed.” No one can say, “Jesus is Lord,” but by the Holy Spirit.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, iheo iria iheanagwo nĩ mĩthemba mĩthemba, no Roho ũrĩa ũciheanaga no ũmwe.
English: Now there are various kinds of gifts, but the same Spirit.

Kikuyu: O na njĩra cia gũtungata nĩ mĩthemba mĩthemba, no Mwathani ũrĩa ũtungatagwo no ũmwe.
English: There are various kinds of service, and the same Lord.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ kũrĩ mĩrutĩre ya wĩra mĩthemba mĩthemba, no Ngai no ũmwe ũrĩa ũhotithagia maũndũ mothe gwĩkĩka thĩinĩ wa andũ othe.
English: There are various kinds of workings, but the same God who works all things in all.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, o mũndũ nĩaheagwo ũndũ wa kuonania atĩ arĩ na Roho, naguo ũndũ ũcio nĩ wa kũguna andũ othe.
English: But to each one is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the profit of all.

Kikuyu: Roho ũcio nĩwe ũheaga mũndũ ũmwe ndũmĩrĩri ya ũũgĩ, na ũngĩ akaheo ũmenyo nĩ Roho o ro ũcio,
English: For to one is given through the Spirit the word of wisdom, and to another the word of knowledge according to the same Spirit,

Kikuyu: nake ũngĩ akaheo wĩtĩkio nĩ Roho o ro ũcio, na ũngĩ akaheo iheo cia kũhonania nĩ Roho o ro ũcio,
English: to another faith by the same Spirit, and to another gifts of healings by the same Spirit,

Kikuyu: nake ũngĩ akaheo hinya wa kũringa ciama, na ũngĩ akaheo ũrathi, na ũngĩ akaheo gũkũũrana maroho, na ũngĩ akaheo kwaria na thiomi mĩthemba mĩthemba, ningĩ ũngĩ akaheo ũhoti wa gũtaũra thiomi icio.
English: and to another workings of miracles, and to another prophecy, and to another discerning of spirits, to another different kinds of languages, and to another the interpretation of languages.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ macio mothe nĩ wĩra wa Roho o ro ũcio ũmwe, nake agayagĩra o mũndũ o ũrĩa ekwenda.
English: But the one and the same Spirit produces all of these, distributing to each one separately as he desires.

Kikuyu: Mwĩrĩ wa mũndũ nĩ ũmwe, o na gwatuĩka ũrĩ ciĩga nyingĩ; ningĩ o na gwatuĩka ciĩga ciaguo ciothe nĩ nyingĩ-rĩ, ciothe no mwĩrĩ o ũmwe. Ũguo noguo Kristũ atariĩ.
English: For as the body is one and has many members, and all the members of the body, being many, are one body; so also is Christ.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ithuothe twabatithirio na Roho ũmwe tũgĩtuĩka a mwĩrĩ ũmwe, o na tũrĩ Ayahudi kana tũrĩ Ayunani, tũrĩ ngombo o na kana tũtarĩ ngombo, na ithuothe tũkĩnyuithio Roho o ro ũcio ũmwe.
English: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, whether bond or free; and were all given to drink into one Spirit.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mwĩrĩ ndũkoragwo na kĩĩga kĩmwe, no nĩ wa ciĩga nyingĩ.
English: For the body is not one member, but many.

Kikuyu: Kũgũrũ kũngiuga atĩ, “Tondũ ti niĩ guoko-rĩ, ndirĩ wa mwĩrĩ ũyũ,” Ũndũ ũcio ndũngĩtũma gũtige gũtuĩka kĩĩga kĩa mwĩrĩ ũcio.
English: If the foot would say, “Because I’m not the hand, I’m not part of the body,” it is not therefore not part of the body.

Kikuyu: Nakuo gũtũ kũngiuga atĩ, “Tondũ ti niĩ riitho-rĩ, ndirĩ wa mwĩrĩ ũcio,” nĩ ũndũ wa gĩtũmi kĩu gũtingĩtiga gũtuĩka kĩĩga kĩa mwĩrĩ ũcio.
English: If the ear would say, “Because I’m not the eye, I’m not part of the body,” it’s not therefore not part of the body.

Kikuyu: Mwĩrĩ wothe ũngĩkorwo no riitho-rĩ, kũigua tũngĩiguaga na kĩ? Mwĩrĩ wothe ũngĩkorwo no gũtũ-rĩ, kũnungĩra tũngĩnungagĩra na kĩ?
English: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole were hearing, where would the smelling be?

Kikuyu: No ũrĩa kũrĩ, nĩ atĩ Ngai nĩ eekĩrire ciĩga ciothe thĩinĩ wa mwĩrĩ, agĩciĩkĩra o ta ũrĩa eendaga ciikare.
English: But now God has set the members, each one of them, in the body, just as he desired.

Kikuyu: Ciĩga ciothe ingĩtuĩka nĩ kĩĩga kĩmwe-rĩ, mwĩrĩ naguo ũngĩkorwo ha?
English: If they were all one member, where would the body be?

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa kũrĩ nĩ atĩ ciĩga nĩ nyingĩ, no mwĩrĩ nĩ ũmwe.
English: But now they are many members, but one body.

Kikuyu: Riitho rĩtingĩhota kwĩra guoko atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndirĩ na bata nawe!” Naguo mũtwe wĩre magũrũ atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndirĩ na bata na inyuĩ!”
English: The eye can’t tell the hand, “I have no need for you,” or again the head to the feet, “I have no need for you.”

Kikuyu: Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, ciĩga cia mwĩrĩ iria cionekaga itarĩ hinya, nĩcio irĩ bata makĩria,
English: No, much rather, those members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary.

Kikuyu: nacio ciĩga iria tuonaga ta itarĩ cia gũtĩĩo mũno-rĩ, nĩcio tũheaga gĩtĩĩo kĩa mwanya. Nacio ciĩga iria tuonaga ta itagĩrĩire kuoneka nĩimenyagĩrĩrwo mũno,
English: Those parts of the body which we think to be less honorable, on those we bestow more abundant honor; and our unpresentable parts have more abundant modesty,

Kikuyu: o rĩrĩa ciĩga ciitũ iria itarĩ cia kũhithwo itabataragio nĩkũmenyererwo mũno. No Ngai nĩanyiitithanĩtie ciĩga cia mwĩrĩ na agatĩĩithia makĩria ciĩga iria ciagĩte gĩtĩĩo,
English: while our presentable parts have no such need. But God composed the body together, giving more abundant honor to the inferior part,

Kikuyu: nĩgeetha gũtikae kũgĩa na nyamũkano thĩinĩ wa mwĩrĩ, no nĩgeetha ciĩga ciaguo imenyanagĩrĩre o kĩĩga na kĩrĩa kĩngĩ.
English: that there should be no division in the body, but that the members should have the same care for one another.

Kikuyu: Kĩĩga kĩmwe kĩngĩnyariirĩka, ciĩga iria ingĩ inyariirĩkaga hamwe nakĩo; nakĩo kĩĩga kĩmwe kĩngĩheo gĩtĩĩo-rĩ, o nacio ciĩga iria ingĩ nĩikenaga hamwe nakĩo.
English: When one member suffers, all the members suffer with it. When one member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, inyuĩ mũrĩ mwĩrĩ wa Kristũ, na o mũndũ wanyu nĩ kĩĩga kĩaguo.
English: Now you are the body of Christ, and members individually.
